question.questionString: What is an operating system?
question.option: interface between the hardware and application programs
system service provider to the application programs
collection of programs that manages hardware resources

question.questionString: What is the main function of the command interpreter?
question.option: to get and execute the next user-specified command
to provide the interface between the API and application program
to handle the files in the operating system

question.questionString: In Operating Systems, which of the following is/are CPU scheduling algorithms?
To access the services of the operating system, the interface is provided by the ___________
CPU scheduling is the basis of ___________
According to Gabriel lame, how many steps does Euclid’s algorithm require to solve a problem?
question.option: Less than five times the number of digits
More than five times the number of digits
Less than two times the number of digits
More than two times the number of digits

question.questionString: If a link transmits 4000 frames per second, and each slot has 8 bits, what is the transmission rate of the circuit using Time Division Multiplexing (TDM)?
Which of the following is used in an attempt to render a computer resource unavailable to its intended users?
Which of the following architecture is suitable for a wide range of data types?
Which of the following is NOT a function of an operating system?
The prefix form of A-B/ (C * D ^ E) is?
Which of the following sorting algorithms has the best average-case time complexity?
In the context of databases, what does ACID stand for?
What is the primary purpose of a hash function in a hash table?
question.option: To map keys to indices in an array

question.questionString: What is the purpose of JavaScript in web development?
question.option: To add interactivity and behavior to web pages
To define the structure and content of web pages
To style and format web pages
To manage server-side data and databases

question.questionString: Which of the following is true about the Cook-Levin theorem?
question.option: It proves that every problem in NP can be reduced to the Boolean satisfiability problem (SAT) in polynomial time.
It proves that every problem in P is also in NP.
It proves that the Boolean satisfiability problem (SAT) can be solved in polynomial time.
It proves that every problem in NP can be reduced to the Travelling Salesman Problem (TSP) in polynomial time.

question.questionString: In the context of functional programming, what is the primary characteristic of a pure function?
question.option: It does not have any side effects and always produces the same output for the same input.
It does not return any value.
It does not use any global variables.
It can modify the state of the program.

question.questionString: Which of the following is a property of a B-tree of order m?
question.option: Each node has at most m children.
Each node has at most m-1 keys.
Each node has exactly m children.
Each node has exactly m-1 keys.

question.questionString: In the context of compiler design, what is the purpose of register allocation?
question.option: To allocate registers to variables during code generation.
To allocate memory to variables in the stack.
To allocate memory to variables in the heap.
To allocate disk space for program execution.

question.questionString: Which of the following is true about the PageRank algorithm used by Google Search?
question.option: It ranks web pages based on the number and quality of inbound links.
It ranks web pages based on the number of times they are visited.
It ranks web pages based on the number of outbound links they have.
It ranks web pages based on their content length.
